--- title: Does `PyFixest` match `fixest`? aliases: - /compare-fixest-pyfixest.html toc: true toc-title: "On this page" toc-location: left --- This vignette compares estimation results from `fixest` with `pyfixest` via the `rpy2` package. ## Setup ::: {#590011b4 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd import rpy2.robjects as ro from rpy2.robjects import numpy2ri, pandas2ri from rpy2.robjects.conversion import Converter from rpy2.robjects.packages import importr from rpy2.robjects.vectors import ListVector from rpy2.rinterface import ListSexpVector import pyfixest as pf # Import R packages fixest = importr("fixest") stats = importr("stats") broom = importr("broom") # Enable automatic pandas <-> R DataFrame conversion (rpy2 >= 3.6) # Keep R list classes intact (avoid conversion to NamedList). _list_guard = Converter("pandas2ri-list-guard") @_list_guard.rpy2py.register(ListSexpVector) def _keep_r_list_classes(obj): return ListVector(obj) _converter_ctx = ( ro.default_converter + numpy2ri.converter + pandas2ri.converter + _list_guard ).context() _converter_ctx.__enter__() # IPython magic commands for autoreloading %load_ext autoreload %autoreload 2 # Get data using pyfixest data = pf.get_data(model="Feols", N=10_000, seed=99292) ``` ::: ## Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) ### IID Inference First, we estimate a model via `pyfixest. We compute "iid" standard errors. ::: {#7c267dc3 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit = pf.feols(fml="Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2", data=data, vcov="iid") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: We estimate the same model with weights: ::: {#a903a3ef .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_weights = pf.feols( fml="Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2", data=data, weights="weights", vcov="iid" ) ``` ::: Via `r-fixest` and `rpy2`, we get ::: {#265316c0 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} r_fit = fixest.feols( ro.Formula("Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2"), data=data, vcov="iid", ) r_fit_weights = fixest.feols( ro.Formula("Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2"), data=data, weights=ro.Formula("~weights"), vcov="iid", ) ``` ::: R[write to console]: NOTE: 3 observations removed because of NA values (LHS: 1, RHS: 1, Fixed-effects: 1). R[write to console]: NOTE: 3 observations removed because of NA values (LHS: 1, RHS: 1, Fixed-effects: 1). Let's compare how close the covariance matrices are: ::: {#b51660bf .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_vcov = fit._vcov r_vcov = stats.vcov(r_fit) fit_vcov - r_vcov ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ``` array([[-8.13151629e-19, -3.73885637e-22], [-3.74299227e-22, -1.42301535e-19]]) ``` ::: ::: And for WLS: ::: {#8c1393ea .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_weights._vcov - stats.vcov(r_fit_weights) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ``` array([[ 1.73472348e-18, -1.05879118e-21], [-1.05879118e-21, -1.52465931e-19]]) ``` ::: ::: We conclude by comparing all estimation results via the `tidy` methods: ::: {#10ba265b .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit.tidy() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=7} ```{=html}
Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) 2.5% 97.5%
Coefficient
X1 0.112019 0.017042 6.572948 5.181855e-11 0.078612 0.145425
X2 0.732788 0.004621 158.578261 0.000000e+00 0.723730 0.741846
``` ::: ::: ::: {#442089d0 .cell execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code} pd.DataFrame(broom.tidy_fixest(r_fit)).T ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=8} ```{=html}
0 1 2 3 4
0 X1 0.112019 0.017042 6.572948 0.0
1 X2 0.732788 0.004621 158.578261 0.0
``` ::: ::: ::: {#e65194d9 .cell execution_count=9} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_weights.tidy() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=9} ```{=html}
Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) 2.5% 97.5%
Coefficient
X1 0.123687 0.016975 7.286200 3.432810e-13 0.090411 0.156962
X2 0.732244 0.004610 158.844322 0.000000e+00 0.723207 0.741280
``` ::: ::: ::: {#aea5dfe8 .cell execution_count=10} ``` {.python .cell-code} pd.DataFrame(broom.tidy_fixest(r_fit_weights)).T ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=10} ```{=html}
0 1 2 3 4
0 X1 0.123687 0.016975 7.2862 0.0
1 X2 0.732244 0.00461 158.844322 0.0
``` ::: ::: ### Heteroskedastic Errors We repeat the same exercise with heteroskedastic (HC1) errors: ::: {#eca6b851 .cell execution_count=11} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit = pf.feols(fml="Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2", data=data, vcov="hetero") fit_weights = pf.feols( fml="Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2", data=data, vcov="hetero", weights="weights" ) ``` ::: ::: {#333b1573 .cell execution_count=12} ``` {.python .cell-code} r_fit = fixest.feols( ro.Formula("Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2"), data=data, vcov="hetero", ) r_fit_weights = fixest.feols( ro.Formula("Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2"), data=data, weights=ro.Formula("~weights"), vcov="hetero", ) ``` ::: As before, we compare the variance covariance matrices: ::: {#6aa47de7 .cell execution_count=13} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit._vcov - stats.vcov(r_fit) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=13} ``` array([[-1.32623404e-14, -4.06509270e-16], [-4.06509217e-16, 2.99402769e-15]]) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#9eb96cea .cell execution_count=14} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_weights._vcov - stats.vcov(r_fit_weights) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=14} ``` array([[ 2.62574793e-14, -7.81578603e-15], [-7.81578624e-15, 5.84581483e-16]]) ``` ::: ::: We conclude by comparing all estimation results via the `tidy` methods: ::: {#f9ee497d .cell execution_count=15} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit.tidy() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=15} ```{=html}
Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) 2.5% 97.5%
Coefficient
X1 0.112019 0.017105 6.548962 6.082002e-11 0.078490 0.145548
X2 0.732788 0.004579 160.036098 0.000000e+00 0.723812 0.741763
``` ::: ::: ::: {#1aa7fcbf .cell execution_count=16} ``` {.python .cell-code} pd.DataFrame(broom.tidy_fixest(r_fit)).T ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=16} ```{=html}
0 1 2 3 4
0 X1 0.112019 0.017105 6.548962 0.0
1 X2 0.732788 0.004579 160.036098 0.0
``` ::: ::: ::: {#da5c99d4 .cell execution_count=17} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_weights.tidy() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=17} ```{=html}
Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) 2.5% 97.5%
Coefficient
X1 0.123687 0.019470 6.352618 2.210043e-10 0.085521 0.161852
X2 0.732244 0.005169 141.653304 0.000000e+00 0.722111 0.742376
``` ::: ::: ::: {#ef8e7ad2 .cell execution_count=18} ``` {.python .cell-code} pd.DataFrame(broom.tidy_fixest(r_fit_weights)).T ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=18} ```{=html}
0 1 2 3 4
0 X1 0.123687 0.01947 6.352618 0.0
1 X2 0.732244 0.005169 141.653304 0.0
``` ::: ::: ### Cluster-Robust Errors We conclude with cluster robust errors. ::: {#57bc3060 .cell execution_count=19} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit = pf.feols(fml="Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2", data=data, vcov={"CRV1": "f1"}) fit_weights = pf.feols( fml="Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2", data=data, vcov={"CRV1": "f1"}, weights="weights" ) r_fit = fixest.feols( ro.Formula("Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2"), data=data, vcov=ro.Formula("~f1"), ) r_fit_weights = fixest.feols( ro.Formula("Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2"), data=data, weights=ro.Formula("~weights"), vcov=ro.Formula("~f1"), ) ``` ::: ::: {#b38fad24 .cell execution_count=20} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit._vcov - stats.vcov(r_fit) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=20} ``` array([[-2.46479432e-13, 9.20548936e-14], [ 9.20548927e-14, 2.59982533e-15]]) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#68504042 .cell execution_count=21} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_weights._vcov - stats.vcov(r_fit_weights) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=21} ``` array([[-3.05959522e-13, 1.25302228e-14], [ 1.25302228e-14, 1.33040145e-14]]) ``` ::: ::: We conclude by comparing all estimation results via the `tidy` methods: ::: {#8e2d55b2 .cell execution_count=22} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit.tidy() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=22} ```{=html}
Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) 2.5% 97.5%
Coefficient
X1 0.112019 0.015865 7.060624 4.823750e-09 0.080152 0.143885
X2 0.732788 0.004490 163.215618 0.000000e+00 0.723770 0.741806
``` ::: ::: ::: {#af7a8efc .cell execution_count=23} ``` {.python .cell-code} pd.DataFrame(broom.tidy_fixest(r_fit)).T ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=23} ```{=html}
0 1 2 3 4
0 X1 0.112019 0.015865 7.060624 0.0
1 X2 0.732788 0.00449 163.215618 0.0
``` ::: ::: ::: {#79f5f0eb .cell execution_count=24} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_weights.tidy() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=24} ```{=html}
Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) 2.5% 97.5%
Coefficient
X1 0.123687 0.018368 6.733633 1.566958e-08 0.086792 0.160581
X2 0.732244 0.005266 139.062210 0.000000e+00 0.721667 0.742820
``` ::: ::: ::: {#368275bf .cell execution_count=25} ``` {.python .cell-code} pd.DataFrame(broom.tidy_fixest(r_fit_weights)).T ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=25} ```{=html}
0 1 2 3 4
0 X1 0.123687 0.018368 6.733633 0.0
1 X2 0.732244 0.005266 139.06221 0.0
``` ::: ::: ## Poisson Regression ::: {#22a96f29 .cell execution_count=26} ``` {.python .cell-code} data = pf.get_data(model="Fepois") ``` ::: ::: {#c6b29a9f .cell execution_count=27} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_iid = pf.fepois(fml="Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2", data=data, vcov="iid", iwls_tol=1e-10) fit_hetero = pf.fepois( fml="Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2", data=data, vcov="hetero", iwls_tol=1e-10 ) fit_crv = pf.fepois( fml="Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2", data=data, vcov={"CRV1": "f1"}, iwls_tol=1e-10 ) fit_r_iid = fixest.fepois( ro.Formula("Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2"), data=data, vcov="iid", ) fit_r_hetero = fixest.fepois( ro.Formula("Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2"), data=data, vcov="hetero", ) fit_r_crv = fixest.fepois( ro.Formula("Y ~ X1 + X2 | f1 + f2"), data=data, vcov=ro.Formula("~f1"), ) ``` ::: ::: {#6098d79c .cell execution_count=28} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_iid._vcov - stats.vcov(fit_r_iid) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=28} ``` array([[ 1.28424161e-08, -6.96633415e-10], [-6.96633415e-10, 1.80690885e-09]]) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#dcb400dc .cell execution_count=29} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_hetero._vcov - stats.vcov(fit_r_hetero) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=29} ``` array([[ 2.31899949e-08, -7.84197943e-10], [-7.84197943e-10, 3.26991733e-09]]) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#f20497cd .cell execution_count=30} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_crv._vcov - stats.vcov(fit_r_crv) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=30} ``` array([[ 1.63355399e-08, -1.21542149e-10], [-1.21542149e-10, 3.27686579e-09]]) ``` ::: ::: We conclude by comparing all estimation results via the `tidy` methods: ::: {#352dda35 .cell execution_count=31} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_iid.tidy() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=31} ```{=html}
Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) 2.5% 97.5%
Coefficient
X1 -0.006591 0.042025 -0.156836 0.875374 -0.088959 0.075777
X2 -0.014924 0.011336 -1.316520 0.188000 -0.037142 0.007294
``` ::: ::: ::: {#662183e5 .cell execution_count=32} ``` {.python .cell-code} pd.DataFrame(broom.tidy_fixest(fit_r_iid)).T ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=32} ```{=html}
0 1 2 3 4
0 X1 -0.006591 0.042025 -0.156836 0.875374
1 X2 -0.014924 0.011336 -1.316529 0.187996
``` ::: ::: ::: {#06fb2055 .cell execution_count=33} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_hetero.tidy() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=33} ```{=html}

X2 -0.014924 0.010828 -1.378277 0.168118 -0.036146 0.006298
``` ::: ::: ::: {#37b57fdb .cell execution_count=34} ``` {.python .cell-code} pd.DataFrame(broom.tidy_fixest(fit_r_hetero)).T ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=34} ```{=html}
